steps.title: Match escape from routine
steps.content: The claim says Whitman reflects on the escape from everyday life provided by train travel. The supporting quotation should directly show separation from ordinary duties or routine experience.

steps.title: Reject logistical details
steps.content: A concerns opportunities, B concerns social and picturesque charm, and C describes where the real journey begins and the seclusion of a berth. The remaining quotation directly describes detachment from ordinary duties. The answer is D.
